assertions.text: Output contains at least one markdown table
assertions.evidence: Three markdown tables are present: Cancellation Rules, Return Eligibility, and Invalid/No-Op Transitions

assertions.text: Table covers cancellation rules — includes rows for states where cancellation is allowed and where it is not
assertions.evidence: Table 1 rows: PENDING -> Yes, CONFIRMED -> Yes, SHIPPED -> No, DELIVERED -> No, CANCELLED -> No

assertions.text: The 30-day return window rule is addressed — either as a threshold column, a separate table, or flagged as an open question
assertions.evidence: Table 2 'Return Eligibility' includes a 'Days Since Delivery' column with rows at 15, 30, 31, and 60 days explicitly testing the 30-day boundary

assertions.text: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
assertions.evidence: PENDING and CONFIRMED are listed as separate rows in Table 1 rather than grouped with value set notation {PENDING, CONFIRMED} in a single cell

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Columns 'Cancel Allowed?', 'Return Allowed?', and 'Allowed?' are all present in the three tables
